lines.text: Welcome back to the show! Let's see how long you survive this time.
Lights up, volume up! Another brave contestant steps into the booth.
The stage is set and the jukebox is loaded. Let's make some noise!
Here comes your next question.
Ears open. This one counts.
Alright, listen close now.
That's the one! Nicely done.
Correct! You've got a good ear.
Bang on! The crowd loves it.
Yes! Somebody's done their homework.
Ooh, so close! But no.
Not this time, I'm afraid.
Ouch. That one's going to sting.
Wrong note, my friend. Wrong note entirely.
You're on fire! Keep it rolling!
That's a streak! The band is getting nervous.
One life left. No pressure. Okay, maybe a little pressure.
This is it. One wrong answer and the music stops.
Round complete! Things get tougher from here.
On to the next round. The questions bite harder now.
And that's the final curtain! Thanks for playing.
The music stops there. But hey, what a run!
Stop the presses! That's a new high score!
Incredible! Your name goes up in lights on the leaderboard!
You again! My favorite recurring guest. The signal brightens when you tune in, you know.
Back for another episode! I told the Producer you'd return. It printed a receipt. I'm choosing to read that as enthusiasm.
Ladies and gentlemen of the static, a legend walks the studio floor tonight. Places, everyone! All none of you!
Producer, the scores, please. ... Riveting delivery, as always. Truly, the Olivier of office equipment.
Thank you, Producer. It says such warm things about you, folks, when it thinks I'm unplugged.
What my colleague means to say is: you're doing wonderfully, and it is legally unable to express joy.
Well now — the archive coughed something up. A master tape! Shall we hear it together after this round?
Hold on. There's a tape on my desk that was not there a moment ago. The studio does this sometimes. Let's give it a listen.
It's tonight's broadcast, friend — same questions for every soul tuned in past midnight. Make your answers count. The whole frequency is listening.
